mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and datasources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer to manage resources through Kubernetes Custom Resources (CRs) and want to leverage GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes CRs and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the repository for future installations.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> This installs the specified version of the Grafana Operator.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Grafana instance using a Custom Resource (CR).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> This command creates a basic Grafana instance with admin credentials.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> This will show the details and status of the Grafana instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Grafana instances are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> This command uninstalls the Grafana Operator from your cluster.
> This command deletes the Grafana instance, which may result in data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana instances. This process includes draining any workloads if necessary, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backup the current Grafana instance configuration.
> This command upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Grafana instance is still running correctly after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) may not be installed or updated correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/grafana/grafana-operator/main/deploy/crds/grafana.integreatly.org_grafanas.yaml' to install the CRD.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana Operator pod using 'kubectl logs -l app=grafana-operator'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is created and check with 'kubectl get svc'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized access to Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The admin credentials may not be set correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the admin credentials in the Grafana CR and update if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features, including enhanced dashboard management and improved security settings.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API versions for Grafana CRs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v5.10 or v5.11, ensure to update your CR definitions to match the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get grafana'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
